codeReview.summary: This feature is validation-only: the handoff explicitly says there were no source changes, and `git show 031d5cb` only points at the pre-existing `agent-hardening.test.ts` commit rather than a live-validation code delta. The review therefore turns on whether the captured live-browser evidence satisfies the feature contract. It does not. The handoff skips the required fresh-profile provider-missing check (VAL-LIVE-010) and substitutes an unknown-model probe, but those are different code paths: unknown models are rejected in `AgentBridge.spawn()` before any scoop/context is created, while the real missing-provider failure is thrown later from `ScoopContext.init()`. So the requested live validation was not fully performed.

issues.description: The feature contract requires a real VAL-LIVE-010 run on a fresh/no-provider profile (`validation-contract.md:1132-1136`; `features.json:1490-1519`), but the handoff explicitly skips that check and replaces it with `agent --model totally-not-a-real-model ...`. That substitute only exercises the unknown-model short-circuit in `packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:167-173`; the actual provider-missing behavior being requested is raised from `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:236-240`. Because those paths fail for different reasons and at different layers, the required live-browser assertion was not actually validated.
issues.suggestedFix: Re-run live-browser-validation against a fresh/no-provider profile (or another non-destructive clean state), execute `agent . "*" "say hello"`, and capture the real missing-provider stderr/exit behavior with the required screenshot/log evidence before marking the feature complete.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `dev` service healthcheck still uses `curl -sf http://localhost:5710`, which false-negatives in dev mode because the SPA HTML route only responds when `Accept` includes `text/html`.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: `.factory/services.yaml:24` defines the current healthcheck, while `packages/node-server/src/index.ts:1143-1148` explicitly gates the dev-mode index fallback on `req.headers.accept?.includes('text/html')`. This worker had to switch to an HTML-accepting curl probe / `/api/runtime-config` to verify liveness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `webapp-worker` manual-verification steps still tell workers to probe the dev server with `curl -sf http://localhost:5710`, so future live-validation runs will hit the same misleading 404 unless the procedure is updated.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: `.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:100,108` repeat the raw curl probe, but the server behavior in `packages/node-server/src/index.ts:1143-1148` requires an HTML `Accept` header for `/`. The reviewed transcript shows multiple exploratory curl calls before the worker discovered the cause.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The user-testing guidance does not document a non-destructive way to validate both the provider-missing path and the provider-configured happy path in one live run, even though the contract requires both states.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: `.factory/library/user-testing.md:44,74-75` discusses configured-provider validation and shared-session attachment, but not how to obtain a fresh/no-provider state once the shared dev profile already has credentials. In the reviewed handoff (`...live-browser-validation...json:12,114-115`), that gap led directly to VAL-LIVE-010 being skipped to avoid destroying the shared profile.

summary: Fail. The live-browser run left the dev server up and gathered most requested evidence, but it skipped the required fresh-profile missing-provider assertion and replaced it with an unknown-model check, which does not validate the same path. I also noted service/skill/library guidance gaps around the dev-server healthcheck and fresh-profile live-validation procedure.
